label: FACIS Simulation → MQTT → Kafka
info:
Receives simulation tick envelopes via HTTP POST, validates schema,
splits into individual feed messages, and publishes each to its
corresponding MQTT topic using Node-RED built-in MQTT nodes.

No external plugins required — uses only core Node-RED nodes.

Kafka ingestion is handled downstream by NiFi ConsumeMQTT.

Endpoint: POST /api/sim/tick

MQTT Topics (9 feeds):
  Smart Energy:
    - facis/energy/meter/{meter_id}
    - facis/energy/pv/{system_id}
    - facis/weather/current
    - facis/prices/spot
    - facis/loads/{device_type}
  Smart City:
    - facis/city/light/{zone_id}
    - facis/city/traffic/{zone_id}
    - facis/city/event/{zone_id}
    - facis/city/weather

Architecture:
  Simulation Service → HTTP POST → ORCE (this flow)
    → MQTT Broker → NiFi ConsumeMQTT → Kafka Bronze Topics

Alternative flows:
  - facis-simulation.json:         Validate only (no Kafka/MQTT output)
  - facis-simulation-cluster.json: Direct Kafka via rdkafka plugin

name: Validate Schema
Split & Route to MQTT Topics
Set MQTT Properties
Track Connection Health
Build Health Response
FACIS: Simulation → ORCE → MQTT (No Plugins)
info:
## Architecture (Plugin-Free)

This flow uses ONLY built-in Node-RED nodes — no rdkafka or
other external plugins required.

### Data Path
1. Simulation Service POSTs unified tick envelope to ORCE
2. ORCE validates schema structure
3. ORCE splits envelope into individual feed messages
4. ORCE publishes each feed to its MQTT topic (built-in mqtt out)
5. NiFi ConsumeMQTT subscribes and routes to Kafka Bronze topics

### MQTT → Kafka Mapping (via NiFi)
  facis/energy/meter/*    → energy.bronze.meter
  facis/energy/pv/*       → energy.bronze.pv
  facis/weather/current   → energy.bronze.weather
  facis/prices/spot       → energy.bronze.price
  facis/loads/*           → energy.bronze.consumer
  facis/city/light/*      → (NiFi Bronze routing)
  facis/city/traffic/*    → (NiFi Bronze routing)
  facis/city/event/*      → (NiFi Bronze routing)
  facis/city/weather      → (NiFi Bronze routing)

### QoS Levels (matching topics.py)
  QoS 0: weather, consumers/loads, lights, traffic, city_weather
  QoS 1: meters, pv, price, events

### Monitoring
  GET /api/orce/health — flow health + MQTT connection status
  Status node tracks MQTT broker connectivity
  Debug nodes log tick stats and MQTT status

### Flow Variants
  facis-simulation.json         — Validate only (sim handles Kafka)
  facis-simulation-mqtt.json    — THIS FILE: MQTT output (no plugins)
  facis-simulation-cluster.json — rdkafka output (requires plugin)
